skill.name: diagnostic-study-quality-assessment-quadas
skill.description: Analyzes clinical diagnostic accuracy studies for bias using the QUADAS-2 tool. Use when Claude needs to assess the quality, risk of bias, or applicability of diagnostic accuracy studies (e.g., "Assess this paper using QUADAS-2").
skill.summary: Analyzes clinical diagnostic accuracy studies for bias using the QUADAS-2 tool. Helps Claude apply the QUADAS-2 framework to assess risk of bias and applicability of diagnostic accuracy studies.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 17 high-risk static findings labeled 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' are false positives caused by a defective scanner pattern matching on plain-text documentation and reference files. All 22 medium-risk 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are false positives from the scanner confusing markdown inline code formatting with shell execution. The only confirmed issues are low-risk: a hardcoded GitHub source URL (legitimate citation) and a Python file write operation for PDF text extraction (intended functionality).

low_findings.title: Hardcoded GitHub source URL
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded URL to the skill's GitHub source repository. This is a legitimate source citation, not an exfiltration or malicious endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URL points to the skill's own GitHub repository (github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). Standard attribution with no exfiltration risk.

low_findings.title: Python file write operation
low_findings.description: pdf_extractor.py writes extracted PDF text to a user-specified output file. This is legitimate functionality for the skill's PDF extraction purpose.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: File write is for saving extracted PDF text to local output. The path is user-specified via command argument. No remote upload or data exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm (false positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged 17 instances of 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' in documentation files. All are false positives - the flagged lines contain mundane text like medical assessment criteria and placeholder documentation content with no cryptographic operations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: All flagged lines contain plain English text from medical documentation files. Zero cryptographic operations exist anywhere in the skill. Scanner pattern is defective.

dangerous_patterns.title: Ruby/shell backtick execution (false positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged 22 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md. These are markdown inline code formatting backticks and example bash commands in markdown code blocks, not executable shell commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is a documentation file. Markdown backticks are formatting syntax, not shell execution operators. Example bash commands are inside markdown code blocks and are not executable.

dangerous_patterns.title: System reconnaissance (false positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged 5 instances of 'System reconnaissance' across multiple files. Flagged lines contain medical research methodology text and audit result notes, not reconnaissance activity.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Flagged content includes patient exclusion criteria, reference standard questions, and PDF text section searching. No system reconnaissance activity.

content.user_title: Assess diagnostic study quality with QUADAS-2
content.value_statement: Medical researchers and reviewers need to systematically evaluate the risk of bias in diagnostic accuracy studies. This skill provides a structured QUADAS-2 framework to assess patient selection, index test, reference standard, and flow and timing domains with reproducible methodology.
actual_capabilities: Assess risk of bias across all four QUADAS-2 domains: patient selection, index test, reference standard, and flow and timing
Evaluate applicability concerns for each domain based on the review question
Generate structured findings with signaling questions, supporting quotes, and judgment rationale
Extract and analyze text from PDF research papers using the provided Python script
Produce reproducible assessment reports suitable for systematic reviews and meta-analyses
limitations: Requires the full text of the study being assessed to produce accurate judgments
Cannot verify the accuracy of quoted evidence extracted from PDF sources
Does not calculate inter-rater reliability or aggregate results across multiple studies
Explanations for findings must be provided in Chinese per skill configuration

use_cases.title: Systematic review quality assessment
use_cases.description: A researcher conducting a systematic review of diagnostic accuracy studies uses this skill to consistently evaluate each study against the QUADAS-2 framework.

use_cases.title: Peer review of diagnostic studies
use_cases.description: A journal reviewer or editor evaluates a submitted diagnostic accuracy study for methodological rigor and risk of bias using structured QUADAS-2 criteria.

use_cases.title: Evidence-based practice evaluation
use_cases.description: A clinician or evidence-based practice specialist assesses diagnostic tests or screening tools by evaluating the quality of supporting clinical studies.

prompt_templates.title: Basic QUADAS-2 assessment of a study
prompt_templates.prompt: Please assess the following diagnostic accuracy study using QUADAS-2. Evaluate risk of bias for patient selection, index test, reference standard, and flow and timing domains. Base all judgments on the text provided.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a first-time assessment of a single diagnostic accuracy study

prompt_templates.title: Detailed assessment with Chinese explanations
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this diagnostic accuracy study using QUADAS-2. For each signaling question, provide a comment in Chinese, a direct quote from the text as evidence, and a judgment of Yes, No, or Unclear. If information is missing, mark it as Unclear.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a bilingual assessment with Chinese commentary as required by the skill

prompt_templates.title: Methods section extraction and assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the Methods section from this research paper PDF and then assess the study using QUADAS-2. Focus on the patient selection criteria, the index test procedure, the reference standard used, and the flow and timing of the study.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides a PDF file and needs automatic extraction followed by quality assessment

prompt_templates.title: Comparative quality assessment across studies
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess each of the following diagnostic accuracy studies using QUADAS-2. For each study, provide risk of bias and applicability judgments for all four domains. Then create a summary table comparing the quality across all studies.
prompt_templates.scenario: User is conducting a systematic review and needs to compare quality across multiple studies

output_examples.input: Assess this diagnostic accuracy study of a new rapid COVID-19 test using QUADAS-2.
output_examples.output: The assessment produced structured findings for all four QUADAS-2 domains. For patient selection, the study used consecutive sampling so risk of bias was low. The index test was a point-of-care antigen test with a pre-specified threshold, interpreted by staff blinded to PCR results. The reference standard was RT-PCR, which is the gold standard. Flow and timing had an appropriate 48-hour interval between tests. Applicability concerns were low across all domains.

output_examples.input: Evaluate the attached study PDF for bias using the QUADAS-2 criteria.
output_examples.output: The PDF Methods section was extracted successfully. The assessment revealed low risk of bias for patient selection (consecutive enrollment) and index test (blinded interpretation). The reference standard domain had unclear risk because blinding of reference standard interpretation was not reported. Flow and timing showed high risk because 15% of enrolled patients were excluded from the final analysis without explanation. A structured report with supporting quotes was generated.

best_practices: Always base each QUADAS-2 judgment on direct quotes from the study text and mark missing information as Unclear instead of guessing
Complete all four domains (patient selection, index test, reference standard, flow and timing) for a comprehensive assessment
Use the provided PDF extractor script to reliably capture study text before beginning the assessment
anti_patterns: Do not make assumptions about missing study details - always mark unreported information as Unclear to maintain assessment integrity
Do not use the PDF extractor script on untrusted PDF files without validating the source first
Do not skip the applicability assessment - always evaluate both risk of bias and applicability concerns for each domain

faq.question: What is QUADAS-2?
faq.answer: QUADAS-2 is the Quality Assessment of Diagnostic Accuracy Studies tool. It evaluates risk of bias and applicability across four domains: patient selection, index test, reference standard, and flow and timing.

faq.question: Do I need to provide the full study text?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill requires full text of the diagnostic accuracy study to make evidence-based judgments. You can use the PDF extractor script to extract text from PDF files.

faq.question: Can this skill work with studies in languages other than English?
faq.answer: The skill can process studies in any language. However, the comments and explanations in the output are configured to be provided in Chinese per the skill specification.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for Cochrane systematic reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. QUADAS-2 is the recommended tool for assessing diagnostic accuracy studies in Cochrane reviews. This skill follows the standard QUADAS-2 signaling questions and judgment structure.

faq.question: What does Unclear mean in the assessment?
faq.answer: Unclear means the study did not report sufficient information to make a definitive Yes or No judgment. According to skill rules, you must select Unclear when information is missing and should not guess.

faq.question: Does this skill run code on my machine?
faq.answer: The skill includes a Python script for PDF text extraction that you can run locally. The assessment workflow itself does not execute code automatically and is performed through interaction with the AI model.
